name: Phosphatidylserine
historicalUse: Phosphatidylserine is an endogenous phospholipid with no direct classical herbal history. However, the tradition of consuming brain and organ tissues for cognitive support — documented in Arabic culinary medicine and Chinese dietary therapy — reflects empirical recognition that neural tissue lipids support brain function. Rambam's dietary recommendations for brain health included organ-rich preparations consistent with high phospholipid content.
classicalSources: Empirical parallels in Ibn Sina's recommendations for brain-nourishing foods
modernEvidence: PS is the most abundant phospholipid in brain cell membranes, critical for membrane fluidity, neurotransmitter release, and cell signaling. Sharp-PS® from sunflower lecithin avoids BSE concerns of bovine-derived PS. The FDA has authorized a qualified health claim for PS and cognitive function (FDA Docket 02P-0413). Clinical trials demonstrate improved memory, attention, and processing speed in age-related cognitive decline.
mechanisms: Neuronal membrane integrity — maintains membrane fluidity and receptor density
Acetylcholine release — supports vesicular release at cholinergic synapses
Cortisol buffering — reduces exercise and stress-induced cortisol elevation
Dopamine and serotonin signaling — modulates neurotransmitter release kinetics
benefits: FDA qualified health claim: may reduce risk of cognitive dysfunction
Supports healthy memory and attention span
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Sunflower-derived (Sharp-PS® ) avoids BSE/prion concerns of bovine sources. Well tolerated at 100–300mg/day. Mild GI discomfort at high doses.
contraindications: Anticoagulant therapy — theoretical interaction at high doses
No absolute contraindications at standard doses
drugInteractions: Anticoagulants — additive antiplatelet activity at high doses; monitor INR
physicianNotes: FDA-qualified health claim for cognitive function makes PS among the most defensible ingredients in cognitive support formulations. Sunflower-derived form eliminates safety concerns of bovine-derived products. Appropriate across broad patient populations including elderly patients with early cognitive decline.
patientGuidance: Can be taken with or without food. Mental performance benefits typically become noticeable after 4–6 weeks of consistent use.
